steps.title: Identify the time signal
steps.content: "In 1613" anchors the event to a specific, completed moment in the past. The surrounding verbs (ignited, was gone) are also simple past, establishing the tense of the narrative.

steps.title: Apply the simple-past rule for specific past dates — per-choice diagnosis
steps.content:
A specific past date requires simple past tense. Any other tense clashes with that date.
  • Choice A — "has malfunctioned": present perfect. Present perfect describes past events with a connection to the present; it cannot be used with a specific past date like "In 1613." Eliminated.
  • Choice B — "malfunctioned": simple past. Correctly places the event at the completed point in 1613 and matches the tense of the surrounding verbs. Correct.
  • Choice C — "malfunctions": simple present. Describes habitual or ongoing action, not a one-time event in 1613. Eliminated.
  • Choice D — "will malfunction": simple future. Cannot describe something that already happened. Eliminated.
